0.q: The results presented here provide crucial evidence for the importance of the TPS/TPP pathway for the synthesis and accumulation of trehalose and the decisive contribution of this disaccharide to osmotic adaptation in T. thermophilus RQ-1.

The two sets of genes, namely, otsA/otsB for the synthesis of trehalose and mpgS/mpgP for the synthesis of MG, were necessary for the growth of Thermus thermophilus in a defined medium containing up to 6% NaCl.
The synthesis of trehalose in T. thermophilus strains proceeds via the trehalose-phosphate synthase/trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase (TPS/TPP) pathway coded by otsA/otsB genes.

1.q: The results presented here provide crucial evidence for the importance of the TPS/TPP pathway for the synthesis and accumulation of trehalose and the decisive contribution of this disaccharide to osmotic adaptation in T. thermophilus RQ-1.

The two sets of genes, namely, otsA/otsB for the synthesis of trehalose and mpgS/mpgP for the synthesis of MG, were necessary for the growth of Thermus thermophilus in a defined medium containing up to 6% NaCl.
The synthesis of trehalose in T. thermophilus strains proceeds via the trehalose-phosphate synthase/trehalose-6-phosphate phosphatase (TPS/TPP) pathway coded by otsA/otsB genes.

2.q: The two sets of genes, namely, otsA/otsB for the synthesis of trehalose and mpgS/mpgP for the synthesis of MG, were necessary for the growth of Thermus thermophilus in a defined medium containing up to 6% NaCl.
the biosynthesis of MG involves mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate synthase (MpgS) that converts GDP-mannose and 3-phosphoglycerate into mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate (MPG)

3.q: The two sets of genes, namely, otsA/otsB for the synthesis of trehalose and mpgS/mpgP for the synthesis of MG, were necessary for the growth of Thermus thermophilus in a defined medium containing up to 6% NaCl.
mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate (MPG), which is subsequently dephosphorylated to MG by a mannosyl-3-phosphoglycerate phosphatase (MpgP)

4.q: Via the trehalose synthase (TreS) encoded by treS that catalyzes the conversion of maltose into trehalose.

5.q: Mutant HB27M9 did not grow in the same medium containing more than 1% NaCl [due to mpgS disruption eliminating MG production].

6.q: we constructed a mutant... in which TPS (otsA) and TPP (otsB) genes were disrupted... growth in a defined medium containing NaCl led to the accumulation of mannosylglycerate but not trehalose.
